artifacts.content:
From: Michael Peterson <mapeterson67@gmail.com>
To: Laura Peterson <lauracpeterson@gmail.com>
Date: 2023-03-17 21:42
Subject: Tonight + HR/Dr. Liu

Laura,

I’m finally home and my hands are still a little shaky, but I wanted to get this all down while it’s fresh. The panic attack on the shop floor at the Warren plant this afternoon was worse than anything I’ve had since Mom died. One minute I was walking the guys through the duct routing, the next my chest was tight, ears ringing, and I honestly thought I was going to pass out right there by Line 3.

HR pulled me into that little conference room off the main corridor and basically said they’re officially moving me into a reduced-hours remote role – about 25 hours a week – starting Monday. I’ll still be a Senior Mechanical Engineer / Project Manager on paper, but mostly handling project documentation and mentoring from home instead of being out at job sites. They were kind about it, but it still feels like a punch to the gut.

I called Beaumont Behavioral Health in Royal Oak on the drive back and left a message for Dr. Karen Liu’s office. After what she said last spring about the PTSD and generalized anxiety, I guess I shouldn’t be surprised this boiled over at work. I put a reminder in my calendar to follow up first thing Monday if they don’t call back.

Can we talk tomorrow about what 25 hours really looks like for us? Part of me is relieved – more time to help Dad and maybe actually sleep – but I’m worried about money and about what this means for church, especially Thursday night visits.

I’m going to try to read a Psalm and head to bed. Thanks for picking up the slack tonight.

Love,
Michael

From: Michael Peterson <m.peterson@midwestengineeringgroup.com>
To: Tom Peterson <tom.peterson@gmail.com>
Date: 2024-06-08 07:12
Subject: Rough stretch – work, Dad, and your stuff

Tom,

Typing this before my coffee gets cold and I head over to Grace to print tomorrow’s Bible study. It’s been a rough couple of weeks and I just need to be honest with somebody who isn’t my boss or Pastor.

You already know about Dad’s move to Maple Grove Assisted Living back on 2022-11-01, but the money side has gotten ugly. I’ve cut back to about 32 hours a week at the Livonia office and I’m taking unpaid leave at least one Friday a month to drive to Grand Rapids, sit with the Maple Grove billing folks, and keep chipping away at his Medicaid paperwork. As of December the past-due balance was sitting right around $7,800. I met with Linda in billing again last Friday and it’s still hanging over us.

On top of that, I got a written warning on 6/4 for missing the ASME compliance checks on the Ford Rouge boiler retrofit. Completely my fault – I let the documentation slip while juggling Dad’s neurology appointments and those Sunrise/Spectrum runs last year. Eric Lawson has me on weekly check-in calls now, mostly documenting corrective actions instead of leading new design work. It’s humbling after 15+ years here.

I keep thinking about your second DUI hearing in Holland last fall and how you’ve been trying to rebuild trust. In a weird way, this feels similar – rebuilding credibility one small step at a time. How are the court-ordered counseling and AA meetings going, honestly? I still use that Thursday-night parents-of-addicts group at Grace to process some of it.

Anyway, didn’t mean to dump so much. Call when you’ve got a quiet drive between calls.

Love,
Mike

metadata.subject: Rough stretch – work, Dad, and your stuff
metadata.location: Midwest Engineering Group, Farmington Hills, MI

artifacts.content:
Title: Care & ministry juggling – Rachel, Dad, Tom, work
Date: 2024-02-10

1. Rachel – Royal Oak
- Since her depressive episode and partial hospitalization at Henry Ford Royal Oak in Oct 2023, I’ve been logging in from home on Mondays and leaving the Livonia office by 3:00 p.m. on Wednesdays to get down to Royal Oak.
- Routine: park near Henry Ford Hospital – Royal Oak, read in the car or grade Bible study notes until her outpatient therapy group lets out at 6:30, then grab dinner together.
- Earlier chapter I don’t want to forget: Feb–Mar 2023 stretch (2/1–3/15) when I was staying at her apartment near Royal Oak High School three nights a week after she filed the PPO against Mark on 1/28/23. Different crisis, same daughter.

2. Dad – Grand Rapids
- Maple Grove Assisted Living since 11/1/22; past-due balance hit ~$7,800 in Dec 2024.
- Sunrise at Cascade spreadsheet (since Mar 2022) for Dad + two other widowers from Grace: med pick-ups, Spectrum Health neuro visits, loneliness/memory flags. Coordinate with Karen so we don’t double-book rides.

3. Tom – Holland
- 2020 OWI arrest (7/3/20) and those Thu–Sun stays in Holland (7/10–9/1/20). Now second DUI in late 2021.
- Thursday 7 p.m. parents-of-adult-children-with-addiction group at Grace grew directly out of walking with him.

4. Work – Midwest Engineering Group
- Reduced-hours remote role (~25 hrs/week) since HR reacted to the 3/17/23 panic attack at the Warren plant and Dr. Liu’s PTSD/anxiety diagnosis.
- Need to balance: Eric’s weekly check-ins, ASME documentation, and being honest about limits.

Prayer: wisdom to know when to say “no” so I can still be present for Laura and our own health.

metadata.title: Care & ministry juggling – Rachel, Dad, Tom, work

artifacts.content:
Event: Royal Oak support days for Rachel
When: Weekly – Mondays (WFH) & Wednesdays 3:00–7:30 p.m.
Start: 2023-10-23
End: 2024-06-30 (review then)
Location: Henry Ford Hospital – Royal Oak / Rachel’s apartment near Royal Oak High School
Attendees: Michael

Notes:
- Mondays: Work from home for Midwest Engineering Group; keep schedule lighter in afternoon in case Rachel needs extra check-ins.
- Wednesdays: Leave Livonia office by 3:00 p.m., drive to Royal Oak. Be at Henry Ford Royal Oak by 4:00 to meet Rachel before her outpatient therapy group. Group runs until 6:30; plan to sit in cafeteria with a book and answer church emails (Grace Lutheran) while she’s in session.
- This rhythm grew out of her earlier crisis in 2020 (panic attack and short inpatient stay at Henry Ford West Bloomfield on 1/4/20) and the 2020–21 Royal Oak apartment season (9/1/20–2/28/21) when I rented the one-bedroom so I could escort her to school events and PPO-related court dates at the Oakland County Courthouse.
- Coordinate with Laura so Thursday night deacon visits and the parents-of-addicts support/prayer group at Grace (every other Thurs, 7:00 p.m., church library) don’t overload the week.
- Remember: this is a marathon. Protect Friday evenings for quiet time with Laura and a walk around Willow Creek when possible.

metadata.title: Royal Oak support days for Rachel

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Peterson <mapeterson67@gmail.com>
To: Pastor Jonathan Keller <pastor@gracelivonia.org>
Date: 2022-01-18 22:03
Subject: Stepping back a bit (sleep, meds, travel)

Pastor,

Following up on our conversation after Bible class Sunday. I agree it’s wise for me to take an informal pause from public leadership at Grace from about 1/15 to 4/15 this year. The stress-related insomnia has gotten out of hand, and Dr. Amanda Lewis at St. Mary Mercy just started me on an SSRI. Between adjusting to the medication and the brain fog from not sleeping, I don’t trust myself to lead Sunday adult study well right now.

Complicating things, Dad’s situation in Grand Rapids keeps pulling me west. You’ll remember back in 2016 when I stayed at Maple Grove Assisted Living from February through the end of September to stabilize his dementia meds after Mom died. It feels like a sequel now – I’m back and forth again, and on top of that I’ve been coordinating meal trains and rides from Grace folks for him since fall 2020, plus covering that $1,200 gap once when his long-term care insurance payment was delayed.

I’m also still overseeing the benevolence subcommittee we set up in January 2020. Last March we quietly cut a $650 check to keep S., the recently divorced mom over by 7 Mile and Farmington, from being evicted when she was two months behind on rent, and the needs haven’t slowed down.

All that to say, I’m feeling stretched thin. I’m happy to continue shut-in communion coordination (especially the quarterly Oakview visits) and behind-the-scenes benevolence work, but I’d like to step back from up-front roles—liturgist, Sunday adult class, and Thursday night visits—for these three months.

Thank you for your understanding and for checking on Laura too.

In Christ,
Michael

metadata.subject: Stepping back a bit (sleep, meds, travel)

artifacts.content:
Title: Benevolence & care log – Biggby, couples, Sunrise, S.
Date: 2021-10-25

1. Benevolence meetings @ Biggby (Middlebelt)
- Since Oct 2019, Pastor’s been routing Evangelism/Outreach assistance cases to me.
- Have now met with at least six families at Biggby Coffee on Middlebelt to review past-due DTE and rent notices, usually $600–$1,200 behind.
- Two families ended up with evictions filed at the 16th District Court in Livonia in 2021; sat with them to set up payment plans and prayed in the parking lot afterward.

2. S. – single mom near 7 Mile & Farmington
- March 2021: Benevolence subcommittee approved my recommendation to cut a $650 check toward two months of back rent to prevent eviction. Covered the remainder quietly through a men’s group offering.

3. Dad & Sunrise at Cascade
- Since March 2022 (note for future timeline): coordinating transportation and grocery support for Dad and two other widowers from Grace at Sunrise at Cascade in Grand Rapids.
- Shared spreadsheet with Karen: med pick-up dates, Spectrum Health appointments, notes on who’s struggling most with memory/loneliness.

4. Couples & Thursday group at our house
- Sept 2020–May 2023: Laura and I hosting three couples Thursday nights in our living room (infidelity, porn addiction, pending divorce). Keeping brief, confidential notes for Pastor—e.g., J.’s relapse into porn in Jan 2022; M.’s plan to file for legal separation at Oakland County Courthouse in April 2023.

Reminder to self: all of this is ministry, but I’m not the Savior. Sleep, walks with Laura, and time with Evan in Grand Rapids matter too.

metadata.title: Benevolence & care log – Biggby, couples, Sunrise, S.

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Peterson <mapeterson67@gmail.com>
To: Karen Miller <karen.miller@example.com>
Date: 2022-03-06 19:18
Subject: Dad, Dan & Allison, and the girl staying with us

Hey sis,

Just landed back in Livonia and wanted to update you before the week gets away from me.

First, Dad: Sunrise at Cascade is still the right place, but the mild stroke on 3/11/22 really knocked him back. I’m planning to be in Grand Rapids four nights a week for the next several months, sleeping on the cot in his Samaritas/Sunrise studio so I can handle his blood pressure checks, insulin shots, and runs over to Spectrum Health for neuro follow-ups. I’ll keep sharing that little spreadsheet I mentioned—him and the two other widowers from Grace we’re helping with rides and groceries.

Second, Dan and Allison: I’ll be in GR again from 3/5 to 4/15 to help with overnight care for Evan while Allison goes to weekly counseling at Pine Ridge Behavioral Health for the postpartum anxiety. We’re basically rotating grandparents so she’s not driving back and forth to Helen DeVos exhausted.

On the home front, Laura and I are still acting as informal guardians for S., the 16-year-old from Grace whose mom is at Brighton Center for Recovery. She’s here on Willow Creek 3–4 nights a week. We had that scare back in May 2018 with the shoplifting at Laurel Park Place and the juvenile court hearing in July at Wayne County Juvenile Court, but she’s been doing better—keeping up at school and even helping Laura with Sunday School prep.

All this while I’m on a reduced-hours (25 hrs/week) remote role with Midwest after the panic attack at the Warren plant and Dr. Liu’s PTSD/anxiety diagnosis. It’s a lot of plates spinning, but I’m strangely grateful I can be present.

Love,
Mike

metadata.subject: Dad, Dan & Allison, and the girl staying with us
